title: Podman logs output include blank lines occasionally
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

The output of podman logs sometimes include blank lines but sometimes not. This will influence the podman logs tests, and makes the ci test failed time to time.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run podman logs task

2. Check the logs file and test results

**Describe the results you received:**
Some output from our CI task:

Running: /usr/bin/podman --root /tmp/podman_test397002604/crio --runroot /tmp/podman_test397002604/crio-run --runtime /usr/bin/runc --conmon /usr/libexec/crio/conmon --cni-config-dir /etc/cni/net.d --storage-driver=vfs run -dt docker.io/library/alpine:latest sh -c echo podman; echo podman; echo podman
d2bb102e7aa2eca7d7056856a9ac2d9b215d1300d07db200aeb8c22272383bfa
Running: /usr/bin/podman --root /tmp/podman_test397002604/crio --runroot /tmp/podman_test397002604/crio-run --runtime /usr/bin/runc --conmon /usr/libexec/crio/conmon --cni-config-dir /etc/cni/net.d --storage-driver=vfs logs d2bb102e7aa2eca7d7056856a9ac2d9b215d1300d07db200aeb8c22272383bfa
podman

podman

podman
......

• Failure [5.262 seconds]
Podman logs
/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/test/e2e/logs_test.go:10
  podman logs for container [It]
  /go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/test/e2e/logs_test.go:32

  Expected
      <int>: 7
  to equal
      <int>: 4

**Describe the results you expected:**

Output from logs should not include blank lines and looks like:
podman
podman
podman

I can't get it to happen?

Hi @rhatdan 

I test it with a newly installed f28 server and the output of podman logs still have problem. And following is the results: 

```
[root@dhcp-3-14 libpod]# podman rm test; podman run --name test -ti fedora sh -c "echo podman;echo podman; echo podman"; podman logs test
no container with name or ID test found: no such container
unable to find container test: no container with name or ID test found: no such container
Trying to pull docker.io/fedora:latest...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:e71c36a80ba912dd7a5a9f2f2d6136c148afa19bc7d024bd616b74a0bc7a2774

podman
podman

podman

podman
```

Strange, I am not seeing this, could this be something to do with language?  redirect the output of podman logs test to a file and see if their are extra white spaces in the file or extra \n?

podman is reading the ctr.log file

```
# podman inspect --format '{{.LogPath}}' test
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/df7d92f6488d63b89e2d1355680558cb1205817ba52860ef6d1fa48087f31c89/userdata/ctr.log
```
Could you examine that file to see if it has extra text in it.

Hi @rhatdan 
Here is the content of the log file:

```
[root@dhcp-3-14 ~]# podman inspect --format '{{.LogPath}}' test
```

@mrunalp @mheon Any ideas?

@ypu What language setting are you using?
```
 echo $LANG
en_US.UTF-8
```
Could this be some unicode interation?
Can't reproduce this over here as well. @ypu Are you testing on a VM? AWS?

Hi @mheon 
I use a kvm vm and installed it with fedora 28 release server. The system is updated after install.
@ypu Can you let me access this machine, via private email.

Urvashi could you take a look at this @mrunalp suggests that there is code in kubernetes that we can take that handles the processing of the log and properly handles the F and P Flags, still do not know why this machine works differently then our  machines.  But the way we handle this now is broken.
@rhatdan ACK. @ypu can I get access to the environment as well.
Actually you don't need access you should be able to just create his log file

@rhatdan how do I create the log file? This is what I am seeing in my log file:
```
# cat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/cba13b705e19caa6b02f54b7beefa03e99286c1cf148439256433de3f934ef62/userdata/ctr.log 
2018-07-02T13:12:47.789677819-04:00 stdout F podman
2018-07-02T13:12:47.789677819-04:00 stdout F podman
2018-07-02T13:12:47.789677819-04:00 stdout F podman
```
I hand edited mine.  This was happening on just one machine.
@vrothberg thanks for the info! Will work on getting something out today.
@umohnani8, awesome :) What do you think about moving the code directly into `libpod`?
Yeah, I think that would be ideal. @mheon @rhatdan  WDYT?
I think a `pkg/logs` package would be a good place
SGTM
Fixed in #1403
